 {"id":"gt-8tmz.38","title":"Fanout gate: waits-for children aggregation","description":"Formalize the fanout gate pattern where a step waits for all dynamically-bonded children.

## Current State

Formulas already use this pattern informally:

    step: aggregate
    waits_for: "all-children"

But it is not in the algebra spec or implemented in the cooker.

## Proposed Syntax

    step: aggregate
    needs: [survey-workers]
    waits-for: children-of(survey-workers)

Or simpler:

    step: aggregate
    needs: [survey-workers]
    waits-for: all-children

## Semantics

- Step cannot start until ALL children of the referenced step have completed
- Children are dynamically bonded via for-each (gt-8tmz.8)
- This is the "aggregation" half of the Christmas Ornament pattern
- If no children were bonded, the gate passes immediately

## Use Cases

**Witness Patrol:**
- survey-workers bonds N polecat-arms
- aggregate waits for all arms to complete
- Then proceeds to save-state

**Refinery Patrol:**
- Less common (sequential processing), but useful for parallel test runs

## Relationship to gate

This is a variant of gate where the condition is "all children complete" rather than
an arbitrary expression. Could be implemented as:

    gate:
      condition: "children-of(survey-workers).all(status == complete)"

But dedicated syntax is cleaner for this common pattern.

## Dependencies

- Depends on gt-8tmz.8 (for-each creates the children)
- Depends on gt-8tmz.4 (gate infrastructure)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T14:19:04.255758-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T14:19:04.255758-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.38","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-25T14:19:04.256284-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.38","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T14:19:11.036957-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.38","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-25T14:19:11.148842-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-8tmz.4","title":"Control flow: loop, gate, branch","description":"Implement control flow operators for the molecule algebra.

## Core Operators

| Operator | Syntax | Purpose |
|----------|--------|---------|
| loop (fixed) | loop: { count: N, body: [steps] } | Fixed iteration |
| loop (conditional) | loop: { until: COND, max: N, body: [steps] } | Bounded conditional iteration |
| gate | gate: { before: step, condition: COND } | Wait for condition before proceeding |
| branch | branch: { from: step, steps: [a,b,c], join: step } | Parallel paths that rejoin |

Conditions evaluated mechanically (step status, output fields) per gt-8tmz.7.

## Design Review (2025-12-25)

Analyzed patrol patterns (witness, deacon, refinery) to verify these constructs
can express real Gas Town workflows. Key findings:

### Insight: Patrol Loop is OUTSIDE the Algebra

The daemon respawns patrols. Each formula describes ONE cycle. This means:
- Formulas don't need "infinite loop with burn-and-respawn"
- loop is only for **inner loops** (refinery's per-branch processing)
- "burn-or-loop" = complete cycle; daemon decides next action
- Context cycling is external, not algebraic

This significantly simplifies the algebra.

### Gap: for-each is Missing

The current proposal doesn't include for-each, but it's essential for patrol patterns:

| Pattern | Use Case |
|---------|----------|
| Witness survey-workers | For each polecat, bond an arm (parallel) |
| Refinery process-branch | For each branch in queue, run merge pipeline (sequential) |
| Deacon plugin-run | For each plugin where gate open, execute |

Proposed syntax:

    for-each:
      collection: "survey-workers.output.polecats"
      bond: mol-polecat-arm
      vars:
        polecat_name: "{item.name}"
      parallel: true

This overlaps with gt-8tmz.8 (runtime dynamic expansion). Decision needed:
- Include for-each here as control flow, OR
- Ensure gt-8tmz.8 covers these patterns adequately

### Gap: waits-for Gate Variant

The "fanout gate" pattern (aggregate waits for all dynamically-bonded children)
needs formalization:

    step: aggregate
    waits-for: children-of(survey-workers)

Currently exists in formulas as waits_for: "all-children" but not in the algebra spec.

### What This Covers

With these operators (plus for-each), patrols become fully expressible:

**Witness Patrol:**
- for-each polecat -> bond mol-polecat-arm (parallel)
- gate with waits-for: all-children for aggregation

**Refinery Patrol:**
- for-each branch -> process pipeline (sequential)
- gate for test verification before merge

**Deacon Patrol:**
- for-each plugin -> execute if gate open
- Simple sequence otherwise

### Scope Decision

**Option A**: Implement loop, gate, branch now. Track for-each and waits-for separately.

**Option B**: Expand scope to include for-each and waits-for formalization.

Recommendation: Option A - implement core constructs, create child issues for gaps.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:33.194896-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T14:18:03.357742-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.4","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:33.196543-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.4","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.985527-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-8tmz.5","title":"Aspects: AOP cross-cutting concerns","description":"Implement aspect-oriented composition:
- Define aspects with pointcuts and advice
- Apply aspects at bond time: bd bond mol --with-aspect security
- Pointcuts use glob patterns to match join points

Enables security-audit, logging, etc. as reusable concerns.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:34.128562-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:15:53.16092-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T12:15:53.16092-08:00","close_reason":"Aspect composition implemented: compose.aspects field, applied during cooking. Demo: shiny-secure.formula.json","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:34.130564-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:49.898125-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.5","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:48:18.802195-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.6","title":"Selection operators: glob, filter, children","description":"Implement selection operators for targeting:
- step(id) - specific step
- glob(pattern) - pattern match (*.implement)
- filter(predicate) - status/output predicates
- children(step), descendants(step) - tree traversal

Used by advice, expansion, and aspects for targeting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:35.447482-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:10:31.184745-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T12:10:31.184745-08:00","close_reason":"Core selection operators implemented: MatchGlob (glob patterns), MatchPointcut (type, label), MatchAnyPointcut. Advanced operators (filter, children, descendants) can be added as needed.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.6","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:35.449213-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-8tmz.7","title":"Condition evaluator for gates and loops","description":"Implement mechanical condition evaluation:
- step.status == 'complete'
- step.output.field == value
- children(step).all(status == 'complete')
- file.exists(path), env.VAR

Keep decidable: no arbitrary code, bounded evaluation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:36.820385-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T13:54:36.414222-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T13:54:36.414222-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented condition evaluator in beads formula package","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-8tmz.7","depends_on_id":"gt-8tmz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T18:04:36.820807-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-i4kq","title":"Update templates for Propulsion Principle","description":"Overhaul agent prompts to embody the Universal Gas Town Propulsion Principle:

> If you find something on your hook, YOU RUN IT.

Templates to update:
- [ ] deacon.md.tmpl - Check hook first, no decision logic
- [ ] polecat.md.tmpl - Propulsion startup, follow molecule
- [ ] witness.md.tmpl - Sling wisps when spawning agents
- [ ] refinery.md.tmpl - Accept slung epics

Key changes:
1. Remove 'should I run this?' decision points
2. Add 'check your hook' as step 1 of startup
3. Make molecule-following the default mode
4. Simplify - agents don't think, they execute","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"gastown/slit","created_at":"2025-12-22T03:17:46.464968-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:05.155061-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T23:44:05.155061-08:00","close_reason":"All four templates updated with Propulsion Principle","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-i4kq","depends_on_id":"gt-4ev4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.245621-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-i4kq","depends_on_id":"gt-uym5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T12:10:42.320803-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-i4lo","title":"mol-polecat-work","description":"Full polecat lifecycle from assignment to decommission.

This proto enables nondeterministic idempotence for polecat work.
A polecat that crashes after any step can restart, read its molecule state,
and continue from the last completed step. No work is lost.

Variables:
- gt-test123 - The source issue ID being worked on","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T21:48:26.320963-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T11:13:37.598677-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T11:13:37.598677-08:00","close_reason":"Stale test molecule from Dec 21 (gt-test123)"}

 {"id":"gt-i6k1","title":"Clean up duplicate patrol protos (gt-qflq, gt-iep9)","description":"There are duplicate protos for patrol molecules:

## Duplicates

| Old Proto | New Proto | Name |
|-----------|-----------|------|
| gt-qflq | mol-witness-patrol | mol-witness-patrol |
| gt-iep9 | mol-deacon-patrol | mol-deacon-patrol |

The old gt-* prefix protos were created before the formula cooking system.
The new mol-* prefix protos were created by `bd cook`.

## Action

1. Close or delete the old gt-* protos
2. Update any references to use the new mol-* protos
3. Verify `bd mol list` shows clean output

## Root Cause

The bd cook command uses the formula name as the proto ID (mol-*), 
not the project prefix (gt-*). This is probably correct behavior,
but means we have legacy protos to clean up.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T13:50:11.424341-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T14:05:53.23666-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T14:05:53.23666-08:00","close_reason":"Closed gt-qflq as superseded by mol-witness-patrol"}
 {"id":"gt-ia0s","title":"Review: Beads Universal Data Plane documentation","description":"Review new docs/beads-data-plane.md (275 lines) for accuracy.

## Commit
- 54c8269: Add Beads Universal Data Plane documentation

## Review focus
- Accuracy of beads architecture description
- Correct field mappings (mail → beads fields)
- Two-level architecture (town vs rig beads) correctly explained","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:49.410707-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T14:27:49.410707-08:00"}
